source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crane/Lifting Device

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Alaminos Canyon Block 857

event.top_event: Personnel struck by flange released from bench vise
actions_taken: Mechanic medevaced to medical facility for stitches
Mechanic returned to rig the next day
Defective vice replaced due to excessive wear on jaw
H&P 205 management addressed issues and submitted corrective actions fleet wide
event.summary: On 08-FEB-2016 at 1315 hours, an H&P Mechanic was struck in the face by a flange associated with a butterfly valve while tightening bolts on a flange connection secured in a bench vise. The flange rotated and released from the vise due to excessive force applied during torqueing of the second-to-last bolt. The mechanic sustained lacerations above the right eyebrow and down the bridge of the nose, requiring medical evacuation and stitches. Contributing factors included improper body positioning, excessive force applied to the tool, and excessive wear on the vice jaw.
recommendations: Personnel should not apply excessive force to a tool while pulling toward their body
Personnel should not stand in the direct path of impact should equipment fail
Proper tool selection should be used for the job to be conducted
Proper body positioning must be maintained during tool use

hazards.name: Pressurized or spring-loaded equipment under tension
Manual tool use with high force application

threats.name: Flange slips or rotates out of vise due to excessive torque force
Personnel positioned in direct line of fire of released object
Worn vise jaw loses clamping force on secured object

consequences.name: Personnel struck by flange causing facial laceration

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for flange connection work
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and safe work procedures

supporting_text: The Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for the work to be done on the flange connection to the butterfly valve was filled out and signed by the rig Mechanic. Steps five and six of the JSA recommend proper body positioning and proper tool selection for the job to be conducted.
The JSA form completed on February 8, 2016, states in steps five and six the need for correct body positioning and proper tool use to prevent injury.

controls.name: Proper body positioning during tool use
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent personnel from being in line of fire
controls.lod_basis: Safe work practice and JSA requirement

supporting_text: Factors which caused the injury include improper body position and excessive force applied to the tool being utilized.
The injured party was positioned to the side of the vice, and was pulling towards his body when the flange slipped out of the vice.
Personnel should not apply excessive force to a tool while pulling toward their body or stand in the direct path of impact should equipment fail.

controls.name: Proper tool selection and use
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure appropriate tools are used for the task
controls.lod_basis: Safe work practice and JSA requirement

supporting_text: Proper tool selection would have prevented this injury.
Steps five and six of the JSA recommend proper body positioning and proper tool selection for the job to be conducted.

controls.name: Equipment maintenance and inspection of bench vise
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to ensure equipment is fit for purpose

supporting_text: The vice utilized during the incident was replaced due to excessive wear on the jaw.
Vice had excessive wear on the jaws, it was replaced.
Standing in line with the open part of the jaw reduces the vice's clamping force on the object being held, and may allow the object to be released directly toward personnel.

controls.name: Corrective actions and fleet-wide management response
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent recurrence across fleet

supporting_text: H & P 205 management have addressed the issues related to the incident and submitted corrective actions fleet wide.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
